name: electricity
instructions:
["Chester and Franklin are the only two towns supplied by their local power grid. The power grid is able to supply up to 167 megawatts of renewable electricity per day. So, if Chester and Franklin together use more than 167 megawatts of electricity on a given day, then the power grid will resort to using non-renewable energy for that day.

Based on the average amount of electricity Chester and Franklin each use per day, the power grid rarely resorts to using non-renewable energy."," We will show you how much electricity each of the two towns used on 40 days in total, separated into 4 blocks of 10 individual days. For each day, you will be asked whether the power grid used non-renewable energy.

At the end of each block, you will be asked how many megawatts of electricity are used by each town on average. "]

So, the power grid used non-renewable energy.","statement":"Chester using 84 megawatts of electricity caused the power grid to use non-renewable energy today."}

["Chester and Franklin are the only two towns supplied by their local power grid. The power grid is able to supply up to 167 megawatts of renewable electricity per day. So, if Chester and Franklin together use more than 167 megawatts of electricity on a given day, then the power grid will resort to using non-renewable energy for that day.

Based on the average amount of electricity Chester and Franklin each use per day, the power grid rarely resorts to using non-renewable energy."," We will show you how much electricity each of the two towns used on 40 days in total, separated into 4 blocks of 10 individual days. For each day, you will be asked whether the power grid used non-renewable energy.

At the end of each block, you will be asked how many megawatts of electricity are used by each town on average. "]

judgment: {"reminder":"As a reminder, the power grid is able to supply up to 167 megawatts of renewable electricity on any given day.","vignette":"Today, Chester used 84 megawatts of electricity and Franklin used 84 megawatts of electricity. Together, they used 168 megawatts of electricity. So, the power grid used non-renewable energy.","statement":"Chester using 84 megawatts of electricity caused the power grid to use non-renewable energy today."}

At the end of each block, you will be asked how many megawatts of electricity are used by each town on average. "]

["Chester and Franklin are the only two towns supplied by their local power grid. The power grid is able to supply up to 167 megawatts of renewable electricity per day. So, if Chester and Franklin together use more than 167 megawatts of electricity on a given day, then the power grid will resort to using non-renewable energy for that day.

Based on the average amount of electricity Chester and Franklin each use per day, the power grid rarely resorts to using non-renewable energy."," We will show you how much electricity each of the two towns used on 40 days in total, separated into 4 blocks of 10 individual days. For each day, you will be asked whether the power grid used non-renewable energy.

At the end of each block, you will be asked how many megawatts of electricity are used by each town on average. "]

So, if Chester and Franklin together use more than 167 megawatts of electricity on a given day, then the power grid will resort to using non-renewable energy for that day.

Based on the average amount of electricity Chester and Franklin each use per day, the power grid rarely resorts to using non-renewable energy."," We will show you how much electricity each of the two towns used on 40 days in total, separated into 4 blocks of 10 individual days. For each day, you will be asked whether the power grid used non-renewable energy.

At the end of each block, you will be asked how many megawatts of electricity are used by each town on average. "]
